message:
test(fcp-registry): drop stale base_manifest_toml string-patch (br-giw5h)

Closes flywheel_connectors-giw5h.

Reviewer (cod_3 P3) found that crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:2546-2556
was hard-coded to patch a pre-9c3a290e shape of the shared minimal
manifest fixture:

  raw.replacen(
    "required = [\"network.dns\"]\noptional = []",
    "required = [\"network.dns\"]\noptional = [\"minimal.op\"]",
    1,
  );
  assert_ne!(patched, raw, "minimal manifest fixture drifted; update ...");

After commit 9c3a290e moved `minimal.op` directly into the fixture's
`required` list, the search pattern no longer existed. The replacen
became a no-op and the assert_ne! tripped on every test that reached
base_manifest_toml — so registry tests aborted at the drift assert
instead of exercising registry logic.

Fix: stop string-patching. The fixture already declares both
`network.dns` and `minimal.op` as required; the three call sites that
look at capabilities (lib.rs:1651-1652, :10385-10386, :11191-11192) all
merge required + optional, so moving the cap from optional → required
is semantically equivalent. Just read the fixture verbatim and
substitute PLACEHOLDER_HASH with a freshly-computed interface hash.

Added a defensive assert that the fixture still contains
PLACEHOLDER_HASH so future fixture drift is loud, not silent.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-registry --lib went from 354 → 356 pass
with the same 6 pre-existing unrelated failures (mock_registry_*,
metamorphic_install_verify — ObjectStore::AlreadyExists; manifest_
signing_bytes_different_manifests_differ).

Cod_4 / SunnyMoose
